root_text.sn51.13:1.1: “Chandañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nissāya labhati samādhiṁ, labhati cittassa ekaggataṁ—
root_text.sn51.13:1.3: So anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:1.4: Uppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:1.5: Anuppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ uppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:1.6: Uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:1.8: Iti ayañca chando, ayañca chandasamādhi, ime ca padhānasaṅkhārā—
root_text.sn51.13:2.1: Vīriyañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nissāya labhati samādhiṁ, labhati cittassa ekaggataṁ—
root_text.sn51.13:2.4: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:2.6: Iti idañca vīriyaṁ, ayañca vīriyasamādhi, ime ca padhānasaṅkhārā—
root_text.sn51.13:3.1: Cittañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nissāya labhati samādhiṁ, labhati cittassa ekaggataṁ—
root_text.sn51.13:3.4: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:3.6: Iti idañca cittaṁ, ayañca cittasamādhi, ime ca padhānasaṅkhārā—
root_text.sn51.13:4.1: Vīmaṁsañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nissāya labhati samādhiṁ, labhati cittassa ekaggataṁ—
root_text.sn51.13:4.3: So anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati …pe…
root_text.sn51.13:4.4: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn51.13:4.6: Iti ayañca vīmaṁsā, ayañca vīmaṁsāsamādhi, ime ca padhānasaṅkhārā—

translation_text.sn51.13:1.1: “Mendicants, if a mendicant depends on enthusiasm in order to gain immersion, gain unification of mind,
translation_text.sn51.13:1.2: this is called immersion due to enthusiasm.
translation_text.sn51.13:1.3: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise.
translation_text.sn51.13:1.4: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen are given up.
translation_text.sn51.13:1.5: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities arise.
translation_text.sn51.13:1.6: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development.
translation_text.sn51.13:1.8: And so there is this enthusiasm, this immersion due to enthusiasm, and these active efforts.
translation_text.sn51.13:1.9: This is called the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.13:2.1: If a mendicant depends on energy in order to gain immersion, gain unification of mind,
translation_text.sn51.13:2.2: this is called immersion due to energy.
translation_text.sn51.13:2.3: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise …
translation_text.sn51.13:2.4: so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development.
translation_text.sn51.13:2.6: And so there is this energy, this immersion due to energy, and these active efforts.
translation_text.sn51.13:2.7: This is called the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.13:3.1: If a mendicant depends on mental development in order to gain immersion, gain unification of mind,
translation_text.sn51.13:3.2: this is called immersion due to mental development.
translation_text.sn51.13:3.3: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise …
translation_text.sn51.13:3.4: so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development.
translation_text.sn51.13:3.6: And so there is this mental development, this immersion due to mental development, and these active efforts.
translation_text.sn51.13:3.7: This is called the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to mental development, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.13:4.1: If a mendicant depends on inquiry in order to gain immersion, gain unification of mind,
translation_text.sn51.13:4.2: this is called immersion due to inquiry.
translation_text.sn51.13:4.3: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise …
translation_text.sn51.13:4.4: so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development.
translation_text.sn51.13:4.6: And so there is this inquiry, this immersion due to inquiry, and these active efforts.
translation_text.sn51.13:4.7: This is called the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to inquiry, and active effort.”
